text:
BOTTOM LINE — Broad risk-on, cyclicals led, energy dumped, breadth decent. SPY gained 0.55% with 62.5% of names up as flows rotated toward software, cloud, and semis while defensives, banks, and utilities lagged despite a VIX at 16.3 sitting below its 20-day average of 17.4. XLK and XLV leadership aligned with single-name winners like Micron, Snowflake, Cloudflare, Palantir, and Thermo Fisher, while oil’s -4.36% slide and Energy 7’s -0.33% underscored de-risking in commodities and old energy. Macro was a non-story versus stock-specific AI and healthcare catalysts, with positioning still hedged via elevated SPY and QQQ put-call ratios despite a Fear & Greed reading of 61.6 in Greed territory.

INDICES — VIX closed at 16.3, down versus its 20-day MA of 17.4 and well off the 52-week high of 31.0. QQQ +0.84%, IWM +0.57%, SPY +0.55%, DIA +0.03%, with STYLE showing LARGE > SMALL by 0.27pp on a day when SLV led major ETFs at +1.27% and USO crashed -4.36% as the standout cross-asset move. SPY remains above its 200-day, so no CTA 200-day sell trigger is active here.

BREADTH — 55.3% of the tape sits above the 20-day, 59.6% above the 50-day, and 65.4% above the 200-day, with 130 advancers versus 78 decliners, 62.5% of names green, and uvol/dvol at 1.57 signaling solid upside participation. Mag7 median +0.53% versus ex-Mag7 +0.58% with narrow_leadership=False, SPY +0.55% vs universe median +0.57%, and mega_cap_tilt=False, so no “index strong, internals weak” divergence today.

VOLUME — SPY proxy IVV traded at 4.6x average volume with price +0.53%, while QQQ volume is inferred elevated via VXN at 23.4 just under its 20-day MA of 23.6, indicating accumulation rather than forced selling. Indices volume breadth shows modest attention in IJH 1.1x and IJR 1.0x versus ignored equal-weight RSP at 0.6x, while sector flows favored XLE and XLF at 1.2x with prices negative, signaling distribution in energy and financials.

SECTORS — XLV Health Care +1.40%, XLK Technology +1.31%, XLY Discretionary +0.42%, XLC Comms +0.35%, XLB Materials +0.35%, XLE Energy -0.07%, XLP Staples -0.18%, XLI Industrials -0.29%, XLF Financials -0.29%, XLRE Real Estate -0.49%, XLU Utilities -1.13%. Top industries were MSOS +10.11% and ARKG +6.43%, while KWEB -1.41% and IBIT -2.10% were the weakest industry ETFs.

ROTATION — Money-in clearly favored XLV and XLK plus high-beta growth industries like ARKK +3.93% and UNG +6.35%, while money rotated out of XLE, XLU, XLF and bottom industries like XES -1.68% and BOAT -2.02%. Defensives_minus_cyclicals at -0.60pp prints a cyclical lean, so label today’s rotation as broad risk-on.

THE 7s — Software +3.59%, Cloud +3.40%, New Metals +2.24%, Semi +1.80%, Old Metals +1.71%; Bank -0.19%, Housing -0.20%, Buildout -0.24%, Energy -0.33%, Old Staples -0.64%. Software strength was led by Snowflake inside software and Cloudflare in cloud, while Energy weakness was anchored by XLE and Freight -1.35% saw its worst name dragging transports.

FED & LIQUIDITY — Next FOMC is June 17 with CME probabilities at 100% hold, 0% cut, 0% hike, and the committee skewed slightly hawkish at 6 hawks, 5 neutral, 4 doves. USD liquidity stress printed 51/100 (Elevated) with CP−Tbill spread at 0.16% in the 86th percentile as the most-stressed component, while TED spread at 0.09% and ON-RRP at $1.9B stayed benign relative to recent history.

POSITIONING — SPY PCR_vol at 1.19 and QQQ PCR_vol at 1.35 reflect active downside hedging, while IWM PCR_vol 2.36 underscores aggressive small-cap protection. Positive IV skew across SPY (+3.3%), QQQ (+8.0%), and GLD (+5.2%) plus VIX below its 20-day and MOVE at 70.9 under its 20-day 75.0 show options markets pricing tail-risk carefully but without panic.

CROSS-ASSET — Oil’s -4.36% USO dump with OVX at 60.0 below its 20-day 72.6 but still historically high weighed on XLE -0.07% and Energy 7 -0.33% even as cyclicals in UNG +2.47% and New Metals +2.24% found buyers. Gold -1.33% and SLV -3.18% selling despite a neutral UUP 0.00% points to position-clearing in precious metals rather than a dollar shock.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed at 61.6 sits solidly in Greed. Biggest single-name upside movers in the tape were MSOS components, Thermo Fisher +7.09% in medtech, and Palantir +7.37% in software, while downside leadership sat in IBIT-linked crypto and KWEB-linked China tech; 20-day momentum is best in XLK +17.15% and Semi +27.57%, worst in XLU -4.74% and Housing -5.24%, with vol-spike names like SOCL 3.7x vol and +1.32% signaling accumulation in social media. UBS’s Micron call and Snowflake’s earnings plus AWS spend commitment were the core catalysts behind the cloud and semi bid.

WATCH — Thermo Fisher (TMO) +7.09% on volume and guidance stands out as a potential medtech trend inflection. Palantir (PLTR) +7.37% with strong momentum in software and elevated skew is a candidate for follow-through or sharp mean reversion depending on dealer positioning around its gamma levels. Cloudflare (NET) +8.3% earlier this week with today’s action around SOCL 3.7x vol and +1.32% keeps the broader cloud/software complex in play for continuation or fade setups.
